channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The 1950 Jaguar Mark V Drophead Coupe: Icon of British Post-War Automotive Design
item.description: The 1950 Jaguar Mark V Drophead Coupe represents one of the most refined examples of British luxury in the post-war era. Released at a critical time for Jaguar, the Mark V combined the elegance of pre...

item.title: Charity Event in 1982: Americans Smash Japanese Car to Support Laid-Off Steelworkers
item.description: In February 1982, a notable protest-cum-charity event took place in northern Indiana, where two American businessmen, Jim Coleman and Charlie Cobb, publicly destroyed a Japanese-made car. This act was...

item.title: ICC Registry Updates on Duterte’s Detention and Health Monitoring
item.description: Former President Rodrigo Duterte is currently being monitored under cell observation at The Hague due to his status as an elderly detainee, as per the International Criminal Court (ICC) Registry. This...

item.title: Pakistan condemns Israel’s detention and mistreatment of Gaza-bound humanitarian activists
item.description: The Foreign Office of Pakistan on Thursday issued a strong condemnation against Israel for the interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters. The FO highlighted the arbitrary deten...

item.title: Eliud Kipchoge Backs Cape Town Marathon’s Bid to Join World Marathon Majors
item.description: Kenya's running legend, Eliud Kipchoge, is showing strong support for the Cape Town Marathon to be recognized as part of the prestigious World Marathon Majors. The two-time Olympic champion, currently...

item.title: NBA Playoffs See Significant Viewership Increase Across NBC, Peacock and ESPN
item.description: The NBA's first year under its new media rights agreement has seen a remarkable surge in television audiences. San Antonio's double-overtime victory over Oklahoma City in Game 1 of the Western Confere...

item.title: Guide to Applying for CHED's Bagong Pilipinas Merit Scholarship Program for AY 2026–2027
item.description: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) has officially opened applications for the Bagong Pilipinas Merit Scholarship Program (BPMSP) for the first semester of Academic Year 2026–2027. Eligible stud...

item.title: Unai Emery leads Aston Villa to first European trophy in 30 years
item.description: Aston Villa secured their first trophy in three decades under the guidance of manager Unai Emery, defeating Freiburg 3-0 in the Europa League final held in Istanbul. After a closely contested first ha...

item.title: A Glimpse of Parisian Life in 1968 and 1970 Through Vintage Photos
item.description: This collection of vintage photographs provides an intimate look at daily life in Paris during the transformative years of 1968 and 1970. Captured shortly after the iconic May 1968 protests, the image...

item.title: Tanzania inaugurates China’s Xi Thought Centre to promote educational exchange
item.description: The government of Tanzania has officially welcomed the opening of China’s Xi Thought Centre in Dar es Salaam. The centre is expected to serve as a hub for promoting educational, cultural, and politica...

item.title: Kakamega Man Found Dead in Homestead; Farm Worker Suspected of Murder
item.description: A family in Samari Village, Matungu, Kakamega County is in deep mourning following the tragic death of their relative, George Ombogo. Ombogo, who had recently returned from Naivasha to oversee his dev...

item.title: CSIRO to Cut 92 Environmental Research Roles Amid Organisational Restructure
item.description: Australia’s national science agency, the CSIRO, has confirmed it will cut 92 roles from its environmental research division, reducing the number of research programs from eight to five. The job reduct...

item.title: Municipal rate increases disproportionately impact small and black-owned property businesses in South Africa
item.description: The South African Institute of Black Property Practitioners (SAIBPP) warns that uniform municipal rate increases hit property owners unevenly, disproportionately affecting small and black-owned proper...

item.title: Kelowna man attacked during morning walk, family calls for public safety
item.description: A man visiting from India, Tejinder Singh, was attacked during a morning walk along Highway 97 near Highway 33 in Kelowna, B.C. According to his daughter, Manvir Sandhu, the incident began when a youn...

item.title: England to Offer Free Bus Travel for Children and Suspend Food Tariffs in August
item.description: The UK government has announced a new initiative to support families amid ongoing cost-of-living pressures. Children aged five to fifteen will be able to travel for free on participating local buses a...

item.title: Man dies after alleged arson attack in Pretoria office; suspect arrested
item.description: A 44-year-old man, Tshepo Edward Kabini, tragically died following an alleged workplace attack in Akasia, Pretoria. Authorities report that the suspect, Raymond Mnisi, also 44, allegedly confronted Ka...

item.title: Robert Kearns’ Fight for Recognition Over Intermittent Windshield Wipers
item.description: In the 1960s, engineer Robert Kearns invented the intermittent windshield wiper, inspired by the human eye's blinking mechanism, which allows wipers to pause between swipes. After demonstrating his in...

item.title: Trump outlines underground military and research facilities beneath new White House ballroom
item.description: President Donald Trump has revealed that the new 90,000-square-foot White House ballroom, currently under construction, will include an extensive underground military complex. The project, which has a...

item.title: American Doctor Under Observation in Prague After Ebola Exposure
item.description: An American doctor who was exposed to the Ebola virus has arrived in the Czech Republic and has been admitted to Prague's Bulovka University Hospital for preventive observation. Czech health authoriti...

item.title: Congressional Amendment Proposes Nationwide Restriction on Police License Plate Tracking
item.description: U.S. lawmakers are set to introduce a bipartisan amendment that would prohibit recipients of federal highway funding from using automated license plate readers (ALPRs) for purposes other than toll col...
